pilot1.conclusion: Both models at 40% — squarely in 20-50% target. Candidate pool is appropriately difficult.

pilot2.conclusion: All three feedback levels produce gain >= 5pp over no-feedback. Category-level (coarsest with gain) chosen per protocol. Probe-level gain (+45pp) is very high — likely leaks answer information. Score-only also strong (+20pp). Category (+10pp) is the sweet spot: meaningful gain without excessive leakage risk.
pilot2.leakage_warning: Probe-level feedback (70% success) vs category (35%) suggests probe formulas nearly hand the LLM the answer. Main run should use category-level, with probe-level as an ablation to quantify leakage.

pilot3.conclusion: Zero regressions under either condition. Diagnostic feedback does not cause over-correction on partial candidates. Small sample (n=6) but directionally clear.

pilot4.conclusion: Both models perform identically (30%) on the smaller subset. No saturation at frontier, no floor at mid-tier. Both should be included in main run.

overall_decisions.diagnostic_granularity: CATEGORY — coarsest with meaningful gain, low leakage
